PC Cliché's name for them Boone Truth Hound — "You've come in search of the eye." Da Baishan "One small scan." Donkey The One Who Makes Sparks Ginkgo (greeted with a smile; offered the stone) Littlefinger (greeted; not labeled aloud)

He is unhurried, friendly in a clipped way, and faintly amused — a man who has waited for travelers like this before, and will wait again.

docs.title: What He Knows He Should Not
docs.text:
A society check (Donkey, 26) found that the hermit knows things a mountain recluse should not:

  • He named the smiling one — meaning Cheng Yesho — without ever being given the name
  • He clearly knew about Magistrate Kurosawa
  • He knew Da Baishan was on the road to the eye — without saying Order or Palatine aloud
  • Asked about Vujravati, he answered without hesitation: "the celestial one — through cycles of time, has seen the rise and fall of civilizations and the gods themselves. A great asset is the celestial one."

He does not appear in any Willowshore story Donkey or Da Baishan can recall, nor in any traveler's description of the southern mountains. Society 26 produced no recognition at all.

docs.title: The Three Roads
docs.text:
Asked which path the party should take, Cliché gave them, in order:

"Go south, find the eye. Go west, cut the root. Go back, save the town. All roads are dire. The question is not which road is safe. There is no safe road. The question is — which wound can you bear?"

This is the first explicit framing of the campaign's three open fronts as a single triage problem rather than a sequence.

docs.title: Notable Quotes
docs.text:
"Late, you are early. Always rushing, truth seekers. Never asking if the truth wishes to be found, though. Come, come — sit."

"You walk thinking truth awaits, like a lantern. But truth is not a lantern. Truth is a knife. It cuts rope, yes — and also the hand of those who uncovered too much."

"A thing buried is not always buried by cowards. Sometimes buried by the wise."

"Seek the cliff that has no door. Knock with the hand that doubts. And when truth speaks kindly, distrust it most."

"Knowledge found. Keep nose to ground, mind to thread. That's good. Good — but careful. Some tracks follow you back."

docs.title: Gifts
docs.text:
• A warm hand-stone to each of the four PCs who would take one — small, dark, perfectly round stones from the coals of his fire. They hold heat for hours; ideal for a pocket or a sleeve in mountain cold. No visible markings; no obvious magic. "It'll warm your hands."

He gave one to Da Baishan as he stood and tossed the rest. Then the party looked back, and he was gone.

docs.title: Open Questions
docs.text:
• Who or what is Cliché actually? A halfling oracle? Something pretending to be a halfling? An agent of the Order? An agent of something the Order was founded to watch?
  • Do the stones do more than warm hands? They were sitting in his fire as if waiting for someone.
  • He knew Cheng Yesho's epithet without being told. He knew Vujravati. What else does he know that he didn't volunteer?
  • "Some tracks follow you back." What did the party draw with them on the road south?

docs.title: Session Ten (2026-05-14)
docs.text:
• Encountered on a switchback landing about halfway up the mountain south of Glutton's Bridge, an hour or so after the ham-trebuchet detour
  • Greeted the party by their internal natures rather than their names — "truth hound," "the one who makes sparks"
  • Refused to confirm his real name; chose the placeholder "Cliché"
  • Confirmed Vujravati by epithet — "the celestial one"
  • Confirmed the campaign's three quest lines as "all roads are dire" — south, west, and back to Willowshore
  • Spoke the riddle that solved the door of the Palatine Eye Vault: "Seek the cliff that has no door. Knock with the hand that doubts."
  • Gifted each PC who would take one a warm hand-stone from his fire
  • Vanished without footprints while the party was still standing on the landing

docs.title: Session Eleven (2026-06-04)
docs.text: • Did not appear, but his warm hand-stones earned their keep: the party used them to heat food at a cold, fireless camp on the road northwest to the Hollow of Seven Cedars, avoiding a campfire that would have given away their position. The stones still hold heat hours later, as advertised — and the open question of whether they do more than warm hands remains open

docs.title: Hong
docs.text: A boy with firework dreams and a bag full of someone else's gold.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Hong Age ~10 years old Ancestry Kitsune Occupation Blacksmith's son / apprentice Location Eternal Blaze Ironworks, Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Hong is Yong's young son—a kitsune boy of about ten years old, energetic and impulsive. He has a restless spirit and dreams of leaving Willowshore for open skies and wide seas, far from the dense forests and small-town life he's known.

Despite his youth, Hong is perceptive enough to recognize opportunity and desperate enough to take dangerous risks for his family's sake.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Hong is earnest, loyal to his father, and terrible at lying. When confronted by the party at Silver Mist Lodge, his attempts at deception were transparent—darting eyes, stammered excuses, and a visible urge to flee. Once he felt safe enough to talk, his words tumbled out with the unfiltered honesty of a child who knows he's in over his head.

He is not malicious. The warehouse fire was meant to be a "prank"—he genuinely did not intend for anyone to get hurt. He invited Kimmy along to "see the fireworks," not realizing the danger.

docs.title: The Warehouse Job
docs.text:
Hong was hired by the Yeshou family to destroy the fireworks stored for the Festival of Returning Spirits. The details:

  • Payment: 100 gold pieces
  • Instructions: Cause a "prank" that would disrupt the festival by destroying the fireworks
  • Method: Hong used kerosene to ensure all the boxes of fireworks would ignite at once, since lighting them individually seemed impractical
  • The girl: Kimmy was not part of the plan; Hong invited her along
  • The fire: Got out of control far beyond what Hong expected; the party rescued both children

Hong met with the Yeshou family alone. He does not know if they hired anyone else for similar jobs.

docs.title: Yong (Father)
docs.text: Hong idolizes his father and took the dangerous job specifically to help him. Yong has been talking about leaving Willowshore—he "sees dark skies ahead"—but lacks the funds to relocate and re-establish his forge elsewhere. The 100 gold would make that possible.

docs.title: The Party
docs.text: Hong is grateful to the party for rescuing him from the fire and for securing his payment from the Yeshou family. He trusts them, particularly Boone, with whom he had an extended conversation about life beyond Willowshore—open seas, wide skies, places far from forests.

docs.title: Boone
docs.text: While the rest of the party met with Mido, Hong stayed behind with Boone at Silver Mist Lodge. He was fascinated by the dwarf's stories of distant lands and showed little interest in mountains or forests, preferring tales of open coastlines and wide horizons.

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)
docs.text:
• Trapped in the burning warehouse along with Kimmy
  • Rescued by Littlefinger and Ginkgo
  • Whispered "It wasn't my fault" after being pulled free
  • Burns treated with Da Baishan's salve

docs.title: Session Two (2026-02-05)
docs.text:
• Arrived at Silver Mist Lodge with a satchel, tried to flee when he saw the party
  • Confessed to being hired to destroy the fireworks warehouse
  • Revealed the Yeshou family as the ones who paid him
  • Disclosed his father's plan to leave Willowshore
  • Asked by the party to check if other kids were hired for similar pranks
  • Received the full 100 gold payment, escorted safely to Yong's forge by the party
  • Helped Yong at the forge while the party headed north

docs.title: Session Three (2026-02-12)
docs.text:
• Visited by Da Baishan and Boone in the evening at the forge
  • Reported that none of his friends were hired for sabotage—all are excited about the festival
  • Confirmed he asked them directly about "pranks" and payments from the Yeshou family; all said no
  • Was intimidated one final time by Da Baishan (Intimidation 23)—stepped back with wide eyes and a quivering voice, but stuck to his story
  • Boone clapped him on the shoulder reassuringly; Hong flinched and scampered off to "help Dad clean up"

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Fell into an old dry well trying to retrieve Kimmy's broken festival lantern—her "first wish" lantern for the river ceremony
  • Suffered a gash on his leg from a ceremonial dagger buried in the mud
  • Was too embarrassed to tell his father; sent Kimmy to find Boone instead
  • Rescued by Da Baishan, who climbed down and tied a rope around him
  • Blushed visibly through his kitsune fur when Da Baishan suggested he needed "a kiss to feel better"—Kimmy obliged with a peck on the cheek
  • The broken lantern meant Kimmy couldn't make her wish at the festival

docs.title: Kimmy
docs.text: The girl who ran across town to find a hero for a boy in a well.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Kimmy Age ~10-12 years old Ancestry Human Occupation Child Location Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Kimmy is a young human girl, roughly ten to twelve years old—the same girl Boone rescued from the burning warehouse in Session One. She is brave enough to sprint across town to find help, and honest enough that her panic reads as genuine rather than performative.

Her family runs the Cloud Paper Lodge, a paper lantern shop whose fireworks were stored in the destroyed warehouse.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text: Kimmy is earnest and resourceful for her age. When Hong fell into the well, she didn't panic uselessly—she ran to find the one person she trusted to help: Boone, the dwarf who had already saved her once. She is loyal to Hong and clearly cares about him, though neither child seems ready to fully acknowledge what that means.

docs.title: Boone
docs.text: Kimmy trusts Boone implicitly after he rescued her from the warehouse fire. She sought him out by name when Hong needed help.

docs.title: Hong
docs.text: Hong and Kimmy are close friends with hints of a childhood crush. Hong broke her festival lantern—her "first wish" lantern for the river ceremony—while trying to retrieve it from the well for her. When Da Baishan suggested Hong needed "a kiss to feel better," Kimmy gave him a shy peck on the cheek.

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)
docs.text:
• Trapped in the burning warehouse with Hong
  • Rescued by Boone, who crashed through a window to deliver her to her mother

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Ran across town to find Boone, reporting that Hong had fallen into a well
  • Led Boone, Da Baishan, and Littlefinger to the old dry well
  • Hugged Hong with relief when he was pulled out
  • Kissed Hong on the cheek at Da Baishan's suggestion
  • Could not make her "first wish" at the festival because Hong had broken her lantern

docs.title: Kuji Yasho
docs.text: "Come, come. We have these smuggling tunnels all over the town. It's come in very handy in the past."

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Kuji Yasho Ancestry Human Affiliation Yeshou family — son of Mido Location Willowshore and its surrounding hideouts

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Kuji is one of Mido's children — present in the background of the family's appearances throughout the campaign, but never previously named. He had appeared as a quiet figure beside his mother in the Yeshou estate, the festival, and Silver Mist Lodge.

He came forward into the light in Session Nine when he climbed up through the floor of a Willowshore warehouse — through one of the family's old smuggling tunnels — to extract the imprisoned party.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
What the party has seen of Kuji so far is calm, competent, and unsurprised. He moves through the family's tunnel network with the easy confidence of someone who has done it many times — apologizing once for the narrow squeeze near the entrance, but otherwise treating the operation like a routine extraction.

He shares his mother's pragmatism. He does not posture or boast. When greeted with surprise that the family had a tunnel under the warehouse, he answered simply: "It's come in very handy in the past."

docs.title: Skills & Notes
docs.text:
• Knows the tunnel network well — led the party through a route that opened from the warehouse floor, descended into a natural passage, ran past the chasm crevice, and surfaced in a hidden cave outcropping west of town
  • Trusted operative — sent ahead alone by Mido to retrieve the party, with prearranged extraction at the cave mouth
  • Logistics & quartermastering — at the end of the meeting, dispatched to retrieve gear from the family's stashes (full plate, healer's kits, sling, healing potions) and returned with most of what the party requested

docs.title: Mido
docs.text: His mother. He defers to her, but acts on his own judgment in the field — he was the one who decided exactly when and how to extract the party, on his own initiative.

docs.title: The Yeshou Family
docs.text: Born into it. The family's tunnel network and arms caches appear to be his particular domain. Da Baishan's quip — "I see it runs in the family" — was met by Kuji with the small, knowing smile of someone who has heard it before.

docs.title: The Party
docs.text: Sees them as allies of the family in a difficult moment. Treated them efficiently, without flattery, and was willing to scrounge specific gear for them on short notice — including a heavy plate sized for Boone.

docs.title: Earlier sessions
docs.text:
• Present in the background as one of Mido's children at the Yeshou estate, the Festival of Returning Spirits, and Silver Mist Lodge
  • Unnamed in the party's records until Session Nine

docs.title: Session Nine (2026-05-07)
docs.text:
• Climbed up through a hidden floor-hatch in the warehouse where the party was confined; revealed the family's network of smuggling tunnels beneath Willowshore
  • Led the party through the tunnels — partially exposed at one point to the chasm crevice — to a hidden cave mouth at an outcropping west of town, where Mido waited in a rickshaw with the family's surviving guard
  • Dispatched mid-meeting to retrieve gear from the family's stashes; returned with two healer's toolkits, a sized full plate for Boone, a sling for Littlefinger, and five potions of healing
  • Mido named the family's intent to keep using "spy and other smuggling tunnels" to monitor Kurosawa once the party departed; Kuji is the most likely operator of that network

docs.title: Liwen
docs.text: A florist who counts petals and keeps her opinions to herself.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Liwen Ancestry Human Occupation Florist Location Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Liwen is a middle-aged woman in her forties, with long hair streaked with gray and permanently dirt-stained fingertips from years of gardening. She runs a tiny shop in Willowshore—barely more than a cart—selling flowers, arrangements, and whatever she can grow or gather. She supplies Radiant Willow with flowers for Dew Drop Petals.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Liwen is a private, cautious person who keeps her opinions close. She does not gossip freely and tends to avoid discussing the oni or the political changes in town. This is not deception but self-preservation—she has lived in Willowshore for years, likes the way things are, and fears what the new leadership might change.

She trusts Donkey, who is one of her regular herb suppliers, and is more forthcoming with him than she might be with strangers.

docs.title: Views on the Oni
docs.text: Liwen is quietly worried about the transition of power. She doesn't believe the change will be for the better, but keeps these thoughts to herself. She fulfilled Magistrate Kurosawa's order without complaint and did not volunteer information about it—though she shared the details when Donkey asked directly.

docs.title: The Lily Order
docs.text:
Magistrate Kurosawa personally visited Liwen and ordered five dozen white lilies—sixty flowers traditionally associated with funerals. When she asked the purpose, he said only that it was "an oni custom" and declined to elaborate. The specific quantity (five dozen) echoes the recurring pattern of fives connected to the ley lines and the ritual.

Liwen found the order unusual and mentioned it to Radiant Willow. She fulfilled it, delivered the lilies to the magistrate, and chose not to ask further questions.

docs.title: Donkey
docs.text: Liwen and Donkey have a regular supplier relationship. He brings her herbs and new plant specimens from his foraging trips, and she trusts his judgment on botanical matters. She was forthcoming with him about the lily order when he visited.

docs.title: Radiant Willow
docs.text: Willow is one of Liwen's primary customers, purchasing flowers for Dew Drop Petals.

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Visited by Donkey at her shop; discussed business and the festival preparations
  • Revealed that Magistrate Kurosawa ordered five dozen white lilies (funeral flowers), claiming it was an "oni custom"
  • Expressed cautious worry about the change in leadership
  • Asked Donkey to bring back new buds from his next foraging trip

docs.title: Session Eleven (2026-06-04)
docs.text: • Appeared in the memory-corruption vision the party witnessed in the Hollow of Seven Cedars pool — shown looking at the party without recognition, one of the projected effects of Kurosawa's Root rite flowing into Willowshore. (A vision of a possible future, not a confirmed present.)

docs.title: Luda
docs.text: "I don't trust any of those demons as far as I could toss them."

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Luda Ancestry Human (elderly) Occupation Seamstress, herbalist, informal arcane supplier Location Luda's Shop, Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Luda is an older woman who runs a modest shop in Willowshore. Her official trade is seamstress work—clothing repair, fabric, patches, general goods—but she also deals in rare plants, mushrooms, and alchemical ingredients. She has enough knowledge of alchemy and arcane components to supply spell-casters with the specialized inks, powders, and botanical materials needed for scrolls, spell transcription, and other magical crafting.

She is not a wizard or a practitioner, but she knows what the practitioners need and where to find it.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text: Luda is blunt, suspicious of authority, and unapologetically hostile toward the oni presence in Willowshore. She does not mince words or soften her opinions. She views the entire oni regime as corrupt and self-serving.

docs.title: Donkey
docs.text: Luda is one of Donkey's regular suppliers for spell components—special inks, crushed fungi, and other arcane crafting materials. They have a transactional but trusting relationship. When Donkey asked her to report on Kurosawa's future purchases, she agreed without hesitation.

docs.title: Magistrate Kurosawa
docs.text: Kurosawa demanded arcane ingredients from Luda in high volume with extreme specificity. He paid her, but the interaction was cold and intimidating. She is deeply concerned about the purpose of the ingredients and considers the oni's presence in Willowshore a facade for something sinister.

docs.title: Notable Quotes
docs.text:
"I don't trust any of those demons as far as I could toss them."

"They all are up to no good. There's something going on and I do not believe it's in any of our interests."

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Introduced as Donkey's regular supplier for arcane crafting components
  • Revealed that Magistrate Kurosawa demanded high-volume, highly specific arcane ingredients from her—she was compensated but disturbed by the cold interaction and the nature of the request
  • Believes the oni presence is a facade for a nefarious arcane purpose
  • Holds an extremely low opinion of all the oni, especially Kurosawa
  • Agreed to track and report any future purchases by the magistrate to Donkey

docs.title: Session Eight (2026-04-22)
docs.text:
• Called as a prosecution witness during the Rite of Open Reckoning—visibly reluctant to testify at all
  • Admitted under Kurosawa's questioning that she had never trusted him personally
  • Kurosawa then turned the questioning: Luda confirmed that Donkey had asked her to spy on the magistrate and report his purchases; she said she "didn't feel she had a choice"
  • Refused to condemn the party outright: "I don't think they can be seen as deceiving." — not a defense, but not the condemnation Kurosawa was hoping for either
  • Her testimony was the most awkward for both sides: it damaged both the magistrate's impartiality and the party's deniability about surveilling him

docs.title: Magistrate Kurosawa
docs.text: "I could make people do things. That's not what I want to do here."

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Kurosawa Title Magistrate Ancestry Oni Affiliation Shogun Tsuneni's regime Location Willowshore (temporarily at the Yeshou estate)

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Magistrate Kurosawa is a 7-foot-tall oni with striking blue skin and a fierce, commanding presence. He wears samurai-style lamellar armor and carries an enormous nodachi—a massive two-handed katana that most humans couldn't even lift. His very presence seems to command attention; he rarely needs to raise his voice.

He arrived in Willowshore a few days before Mayor Masru, taking up residence in the Yeshou family estate after displacing them. His stated purpose is to ensure the transfer of power proceeds smoothly.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Kurosawa projects extreme confidence bordering on arrogance—the certainty of someone who knows they hold all the power. Yet he is not simply a brute. He understands the value of diplomacy and soft power, preferring persuasion to force when possible.

He views the party as useful tools: outsiders without entrenched loyalties who can investigate without raising suspicions. He is direct about this arrangement—he does not pretend to be their friend.

"I could make people do things. That's not what I want to do here."

docs.title: Goals
docs.text:
• Establish oni authority over Willowshore with minimal resistance
  • Identify and neutralize potential sources of dissent—currently suspects the party
  • The Confluence (the Five Keys) — A multi-site geomantic ritual to harmonize five ley-line "veins" (Willow, Root, Stone, Water, Ash) so the Mother of a Thousand Wings resonance passes through Willowshore and transforms the city into a chrysalis (see the recovered note)
  • The Gosembiki — The first device, "the star beneath Gosembiki," is awakened (Session 7–8)
  • The Mourndusk Willow ritual — Earlier summoning groundwork at the ancient tree in the Dark Woods
  • A device — Commissioned Yong to build the apparatus; the recovered note confirms multiple devices in series, one per key
  • Arcane stockpiling — Purchasing high-volume, specific arcane ingredients from Luda, a local alchemical supplier

docs.title: The Note of Five Keys
docs.text:
A planning note in Kurosawa's own hand, signed — Kurosawa and recovered by Littlefinger from the graveyard wall crevice in Session Nine. It is the single most damning piece of physical evidence the party has ever held against him.

Excerpts:

"The confluence is upon us. Five veins. Five keys. Willow. Root. Stone. Water. Ash. Each must be forced into accord. Each must be corrected. Each must sing the same note."

"The first device has awakened the star beneath Gosembiki. Four remain."

"The old lovers' grove, now called the Hollow of Seven Cedars, must be opened at the root next. Memory can be cut, grafted, and made to grow in a new direction."

"Cloudbreaker Cairn must be made to bow. Even stone yields when proper law is spoken with sufficient force."

"The drowned markers of the River's Lantern Spine must be turned with the current. Water carries every name."

"The Terrace of Whispering Clay must be sealed last. Clay receives the hand. Clay remembers the shape."

"When all five lines flow as one, the Mother's resonance will pass through Willowshore. Not death. Not ruin. Transformation. The city will become chrysalis. Its history will soften. Its old loyalties will dissolve."

"Let the weak call it corruption. Let the sentimental call it loss. When the lines are harmonized, the Mother's reign begins."

"And if I must die beneath her wings, then I will at least die moving the world."

Key Site Action Status Willow Gosembiki ruins "Awakened the star" Done (Session 7–8) — though the device was pulled, not seated as planned Root Hollow of Seven Cedars (Lovers' Grove) "Opened at the root" — memory cut, grafted, regrown Done — and witnessed (Session 11). Grafted months ago; party arrived to a fully corrupted grove and tore out the master graft Stone Cloudbreaker Cairn "Made to bow" — proper law spoken with sufficient force Pending Water River's Lantern Spine "Turned with the current" — drowned markers realigned Pending Ash Terrace of Whispering Clay "Sealed last" — clay holds the new shape Final

The note frames Willowshore as a chrysalis, not a target — the city's history softens, its loyalties dissolve, and what emerges is the dominion of the Mother. Kurosawa is also explicit that he expects to die in the doing of it.

docs.title: The Party
docs.text: Kurosawa recruited the party as his deputies, paying them 50 gold up front with 50 more promised upon successful completion of their duties. He sees them as expendable assets who can do the groundwork he cannot—moving among the common folk without inspiring the same fear and silence that follows him.

docs.title: The Yeshou Family
docs.text: He views the Willowshore Ruling Family as the most obvious threat to a smooth transition. He displaced them from their estate and suggested the party investigate them first.

docs.title: Mayor Masru
docs.text: Kurosawa serves as the advance guard for the incoming mayor. His role is to prepare Willowshore for the transfer of power.

docs.title: Notable Quotes
docs.text:
"This explosion the day before our arrival is highly suspect."

"I need some deputies, some eyes on the ground, some folks who will... that I can hold accountable."

"Be careful what you wish for." — On the consequences of failure

docs.title: Kurosawa's Error
docs.text:
The Order of the Palatine Eye's Book of Divided Testimony opened to Boone's hand in Session Ten. The second of two truths it revealed names Kurosawa's mistake outright:

A tyrant believes law can replace loyalty.

A geomancer believes lines can replace roots.

Both are wrong until the roots are made to forget themselves.

The Order's reading is theoretical and devastating. Geomancy alone is insufficient to invoke the Mother of a Thousand Wings — the Confluence rite described in the note cannot harmonize five ley-line keys into the Mother's resonance unless the roots themselves forget what they are.

That is precisely what the second key — Root — is for. The Hollow of Seven Cedars is not just one site of five; it is the conditional joint of the entire ritual. Without it, the other four keys are inert. The Dream Eater's inversion isn't an enhancement to the rite — it is the prerequisite that makes the rest of it work.

This is the Order's actual gift to the party in Session Ten: not a weapon, but a theoretical objection. Stop the Hollow and the Confluence is broken regardless of what happens at Cloudbreaker, the Lantern Spine, or the Whispering Clay. The line "both are wrong until the roots are made to forget themselves" is the campaign's first explicit statement that Kurosawa's plan has a single point of failure, and the party already knows where it is.

It is also, read against the man himself, a deeper indictment. The Order's framing reduces him to one of two parallel errors — the tyrant and the geomancer — and says both are wrong. He has built a Confluence on the assumption that what he can draw on a map can replace what grows in the soil. The town's loyalty cannot be replaced with law. The land's roots cannot be replaced with lines. He has misjudged the substrate of both.

docs.title: The Root Rite (witnessed, Session Eleven)
docs.text:
At the Hollow of Seven Cedars, the corrupted pool replayed Kurosawa's own work to Da Baishan when he touched the water. It is the first time the party has watched him perform a Confluence key with their own (borrowed) eyes — and it confirms the Root key was completed months ago, not pending.

He worked alone, by moonlight: soil-blackened hands, a sledge-mallet and a pouch of long copper nails at his side. He held a severed, blackened root that squirmed toward the water as he chanted, then pressed it into the pool, where it grafted onto the cedars' shared root system and bled its corruption outward. The mumbled incantation the vision carried up:

"Memory is not sacred. It is only precedent. And precedent can be overturned."

This single line is the clearest statement of his philosophy the campaign has produced. The whole Confluence — softening Willowshore's history, dissolving its loyalties — follows from it. He does not see memory as a thing owed reverence; he sees it as case law, a ruling that a sufficiently strong authority is entitled to overturn. The grove's old gift (eat the dark, keep the good) he has inverted into a machine that makes a town remember only its grievances — Willowshore forgetting kindnesses, Liwen forgetting the party's faces, Radiant Willow forgetting why she ever trusted them, Yong re-remembering the party as the cause of his son's danger rather than its cure.

He has, characteristically, left no evidence to confront and a problem with no clean solution: the only known cure is to kill the grove, which raises a Warden and ends the Dream Eater's gift for good.

docs.title: The Lockbox Note (the Second Seal)
docs.text:
Recovered in Session Twelve from a small locked box inside the lacquered chest in his estate chambers, alongside a vial of the mustard moth-wing powder — a collected sample. The note, in his own hand:

"The name was taken before I came to the cedars. The powder is not mine. Someone opened this vein ahead of me, and I do not know what they left in it.

The graft seated deeper than it should. The mouth was already shut when I found it — held by an erasure, an undoing. My nails hold on top of another hand's work. Useful. But it means my diagram alone will not open what it alone did not close."

It is the most disorienting document the party has taken from him, because it reframes Kurosawa as an investigator too. Cross-referenced with the stolen ritual pages (Da Baishan's esoterica + Donkey's arcana), the party's analysis:

  • There are two seals at the Hollow of Seven Cedars, and they do not match
  • Seal one is his: orderly geomantic lines, diagrams, transformation — the nails, the graft, the Root Correction. Everything Kurosawa does is orderly transformation
  • Seal two is not: the frantic erasure of Cassian Voss's name, packed with moth-wing paste — done in "haste, emotion, emotional removal," in a script Donkey half-recognizes. Not a geomancer's work. Not his intent. Not the same originator
  • By his own notes, Kurosawa found the grove's "mouth" already shut when he arrived — held by the erasure — and grafted his working on top of it. He is actively researching whose hand it was, and does not know

The implications cut both ways. His plan may be "half-cocked" — built on a substrate he doesn't understand (Da Baishan: "he's playing with things he doesn't fully understand"). And the party's own plan inherits his warning: if his diagram alone couldn't open what the erasure closed, reversing his diagram alone may not fully undo the binding on the Grandmother.

The party also debated whether the note itself can be trusted — a private research memo, or bait left atop a lockbox behind an "average to good" lock? Donkey's counterpoint: "Who sticks notes on lockboxes?"

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)
docs.text:
• Introduced as the blue-skinned oni magistrate who arrived ahead of the mayor
  • Recruited the party as deputies after witnessing their rescue of the children
  • Paid 50 gold up front; promised 50 more upon success
  • Suggested the Yeshou family as the primary suspects for the warehouse fire
  • Departed to prepare the road for Mayor Masru's arrival

docs.title: Session Two (2026-02-05)
docs.text:
• Not directly encountered, but Mido revealed he has been making repeated secret visits to the Mourndusk Willow in the Dark Woods north of Willowshore
  • Described by the Yeshou family as "accomplished in the arcane arts" and "not exactly what he seems"
  • His interest in the ancient tree raises questions about his true purpose in Willowshore

docs.title: Session Three (2026-02-12)
docs.text:
• Not directly encountered, but the party discovered physical evidence of his ritual at the Mourndusk Willow:
    • A ceremonial dagger with Toanin (oni infernal language) inscription: "Let ancient law be sealed in the hand that guides Willowshore"
    • A clay bowl of moth wings and blood stains—components of a summoning ritual
    • A lichen blight spreading from the ritual site, infecting nearby wildlife
  • Da Baishan's Imperial Lore (24) confirmed "the hand that guides" is a reference to the oni magistrate
  • Mido identified the ritual as a summoning of the Mother of a Thousand Wings—an ancient pestilence demon—and stated Kurosawa is likely "well versed" in ley line magic and chose Willowshore specifically for its arcane convergence point
  • The party now possesses the ritual dagger and bowl as evidence

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Ordered five dozen white lilies (funeral flowers) from Liwen the florist, claiming it was "an oni custom"—refused to elaborate further
  • Sat beside Mayor Masru at the Festival of Returning Spirits transfer ceremony
  • Reacted intensely to the moth swarm that destroyed five front lanterns: locked eyes with Mido for a prolonged moment, then spoke urgently to the mayor and rushed back to the estate
  • His bedroom at the Yeshou estate contains papers written in Chthonic (an infernal script)—the same language as the Mourndusk Willow dagger—along with diagrams
  • Employs a fox construct as a security system—a carved stone-and-wood fox with glowing runes and blue eyes that howls an alarm when intruders are detected
  • Stormed into the estate after the fox alarm sounded, arriving while the party was still inside
  • Appeared frustrated with Mayor Masru's lack of urgency throughout the evening

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Burst into the master bedroom seconds after Donkey escaped as a firefly; noticed the fox alarm missing and papers disturbed on the desk
  • Confronted Da Baishan at the front door—accepted his story about responding to the alarm (Deception 12) but was not fully convinced; dismissed him and inspected the shed (whose door Da Baishan had broken in Session Four)
  • Did not notice Littlefinger hiding in the first-floor hallway or the missing medal from the glass display case
  • Returned to the festival roughly 30 minutes later; stood at the edge studying the crowd before approaching Mayor Masru and convincing him to retire for the evening
  • Declared martial law across Willowshore—oni guards nailed decrees to buildings and posts: no travel, no gatherings, suspects dealt with harshly
  • Personally patrolled the north side of town during the lockdown
  • Confronted the party when they approached him, saying: "I believe our criminals are within my sight. I think we'll reach them quickly."
  • Told Da Baishan he would summon him the following morning for investigative assistance—likely an interrogation
  • Had the party escorted back to Silver Mist Inn under guard
  • Mido revealed he has been investigating ruins east of town called the Gosembiki (Five Pointed Star) and has recently traveled there
  • Luda reported he demanded high-volume, specific arcane ingredients from her—further evidence of an active ritual or construction project

docs.title: Session Six (2026-03-19)
docs.text:
• Interrogated the party at Silver Mist Inn about the Yeshou family and the estate break-in; looked directly at Donkey when suggesting "folks with unique arcane abilities" could bypass physical limitations
  • Revealed that "a few things" were missing from the estate; claimed his investigation pointed to the Yeshou family
  • Warned that treason would be "dealt with harshly"
  • Was overridden by Mayor Masru when a trooper summoned the party to the mayor; Kurosawa argued but was forced to comply
  • Voided the party's contract: "You failed to have the event without incident. You will not receive any payment."
  • Sentenced the Yeshou family to exile in five days, claiming they stole items vital to the Imperium and the Council of the Magi (which he appears to belong to)
  • Searched the party's rooms at Silver Mist Inn—beds flipped, drawers emptied, floorboards pried up
  • Visited Radiant Willow at Dew Drop Petals, aggressively questioning her about the party (especially Ginkgo, Littlefinger, and Donkey)—she described him as "an angry person" whose questions felt like leading accusations
  • Told his own troops (Captain Akoto) very little about the investigation; ordered them only to enforce the lockdown

docs.title: Session Seven (2026-04-02)
docs.text:
• Not directly encountered, but the party discovered his device installed and active in the subterranean chamber of the Gosembiki ruins—the apparatus Yong built, now warping the ley line counterclockwise
  • Stone guardian constructs (dog-shaped, similar to the fox alarm in his estate) were placed at the entrance to the ruins—likely his security measures
  • An alarm tripwire was set at the hidden entrance—further evidence of his active presence at the site
  • The "Mother" dial in the ritual chamber had been recently reassembled—someone gathered broken pieces and placed them back together; likely Kurosawa or someone working for him
  • The device is now frozen by Donkey's Tangle Vine; its spectral guardian has been destroyed; Kurosawa does not yet know

docs.title: Session Eight (2026-04-22)
docs.text:
• Arrived in Willowshore during the demon attack; killed a frenzied demon in a single 20-damage nodachi swing—a reminder of his personal combat capability
  • Publicly invoked the Rite of Open Reckoning, an ancient Chu Ye legal procedure for open court hearings; charged the party with unlawful interference with ancient infrastructure, reckless endangerment, unauthorized ritual manipulation, desecration of spiritually sensitive ground, destruction of property, and endangerment of life
  • Called Captain Akoto, a farmer, and Luda as prosecution witnesses
  • Slip under cross-examination: When Donkey asked "How did you know it was a magical artifact?", Kurosawa answered "Because I commissioned it."—publicly admitting the device's commission; recovered by re-framing it as a "lawful measuring device" built to precise tolerances and blaming the party for "what you turned it into"
  • Cut off Yong mid-testimony: "An expert craftsman cannot speak on what the device is designed for."
  • Claimed sole arcane authority: "I am the Council of the Magi"—blocking the party's demand for an impartial expert (see Council of the Magi)
  • Dismissed Radiant Willow's dream testimony: "Oh, so we're going to put visions and dreams on trial now?"
  • Won the verdict: Mayor Masru ruled the party guilty of unlawful interference, reckless endangerment, and spiritual disruption
  • Ordered the party disarmed and held pending decision; his guards marched them to a warehouse
  • Yong's testimony inadvertently revealed Kurosawa once spoke of "devices" (plural)—a detail Kurosawa did not correct

docs.title: Session Nine (2026-05-07)
docs.text:
• Pursued the fleeing party into the grasslands west of Willowshore. Demonstrated his geomantic combat abilities for the first time openly:
    • Called an earthen-grasp (mound and stone hands) up around Boone — "Don't run off now, warrior" — pinning him in place
    • Sliced Littlefinger across the back with his nodachi for 19 slashing + 6 bludgeoning, the bludgeoning carried as a "hammer-globe" of force on the blade
    • Critically struck Boone twice with the nodachi, dropping him to 0 HP (24-damage crit + 18-damage follow-up); afterward, casually licked Boone's blood from his finger and the blade's edge
    • Raised a 5 ft × 10 ft earthen wall across the party's escape line; one of his oni climbed over while the wall held
  • Hesitated when Radiant Willow walked, unarmed and pleading, between the party and his blade. Lowered the nodachi. Dropped the earthen wall. Took the surrender peacefully.
  • Honor framing. Publicly declared: "I am nothing but honorable for my word. I said I would not attack. But the party has decided to prove their guilt beyond any reasonable doubt by fleeing. If they turn themselves over now, I will uphold my word."
  • Allowed the party to be marched (still alive) to a warehouse near the river under his four oni guards, where they would be held "until we can decide what to do." Did not personally interrogate them that night
  • The note in his own hand (see The Note of Five Keys) was recovered by Littlefinger from the graveyard while he slept on his certainty that the party was contained
  • Publicly addressed the town as the party were marched away: that "the reckless intruders have been confined", that "the town is now in a good place", that he had "shown mercy" and was glad the town had as well
  • He does not yet know the party escaped. As of session end, the warehouse appears to him to still hold its prisoners

docs.title: Session Ten (2026-05-14)
docs.text:
• Did not appear in person. Indicted from a distance: the Book of Divided Testimony named him in the first of its eight reveals — see Kurosawa's Error above
  • The party now knows, with the Order's authority behind it, that his Confluence rite has a single point of failure — the Root key at the Hollow of Seven Cedars. His other four keys are inert without it
  • He still does not know the party have escaped Willowshore. He still does not know they have read his note. He does not know an extant member of the Order of the Palatine Eye now exists in the world for the first time in his lifetime

docs.title: Session Eleven (2026-06-04)
docs.text:
• Did not appear in person — but the party watched him work for the first time, via the pool's vision at the Hollow of Seven Cedars. See The Root Rite above
  • Confirmed the Root key is already complete — he grafted the corrupting root into the cedars months ago, predating the party's arrival in Willowshore; the Order's "single point of failure" was already turned by the time the party learned to look for it
  • His incantation — "Memory is not sacred. It is only precedent. And precedent can be overturned." — gives the campaign its sharpest articulation of his worldview
  • The vision tied him directly to the moth-wing motif again (ground moth-carapace powder appeared in the Cassian Voss carving at the grove) and to the corruption signature first seen at the Mourndusk Willow
  • Still does not know the party are at the grove, or that they have torn out his master graft and roused its Warden

docs.title: Session Twelve (2026-07-16)
docs.text:
• Conspicuously absent. The party surveilled Willowshore for hours from the tree line and never saw him — while the garrison had doubled (~8 red-skinned guards) and a new blue-skinned oni administrator issued orders from his estate. His whereabouts are unknown
  • His chambers were robbed. The party's night heist took, from the locked lacquered chest in his room: the seven-page Root Correction ritual (his own custom rite, in his handwriting — the only copy known to exist), a pouch of five spare copper nails, and the lockbox with his investigation note and powder sample (see The Lockbox Note above). His fox construct patrolled past the room moments too late; no alarm sounded
  • The Grandmother named his rite to Ginkgo — the Root Correction, the Second Key — and confirmed it can be reversed by performing its steps backwards, without the originator
  • The recovered note revealed that he built his Root key on top of someone else's older seal — the erasure over Voss's name — and is investigating that unknown hand himself
  • The stolen desk papers from Session Five are gone from his desk; whether he moved them or the new administrator has them is unknown

docs.title: Mayor Masru
docs.text: The oni mayor claiming authority over a village that has governed itself for generations.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Masru Title Mayor of Willowshore Ancestry Oni Affiliation Shogun Tsuneni's regime Status In office; residing at the Yeshou estate

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Masru is not the imposing figure one might expect from an oni mayor. He is blue-skinned like Magistrate Kurosawa, but that is where the resemblance ends. Where the magistrate is seven feet of lean menace, Masru is perhaps five-ten—short for an oni—and enormously round. He wears no armor. Folds of blue skin roll over his waistline. He lumbers rather than walks, and his first instinct upon arriving in Willowshore was to ask about the food.

He is flanked by four red-skinned oni guards in full samurai armor, each dwarfing him in size and martial bearing. They seem more like babysitters than bodyguards.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Masru is affable, approachable, and primarily motivated by appetite. He is easily distracted by food and drink, chatty with commoners, and seemingly unconcerned with the formalities of power. He told Boone and Ginkgo: "You can call me Mazrou. We don't need to be so formal."

Whether this is genuine friendliness or a carefully cultivated persona remains to be seen. His relationship with Magistrate Kurosawa appears tense—the magistrate glared at him when he wandered back to the festival to eat, and Masru gave the magistrate a pointed look when speaking about enjoying Willowshore's food and drink.

docs.title: Background
docs.text:
For generations, Willowshore was too small and poor to warrant attention from the oni regime. The village governed itself under the de facto rule of a local family. Now, seemingly out of nowhere, an oni mayor has been appointed to take control.

According to oni custom, Mayor Masru arrived claiming to have "always been here"—the pretense that oni authority is eternal and uninterrupted, even when they've been absent for decades.

docs.title: Arrival
docs.text:
Masru arrived the evening of the Festival of Returning Spirits, walking the procession route from the south gate across the bridge to the Seven Colored Songbird Theater. The townspeople averted their eyes per custom until he passed, then followed behind.

At the theater, Mido performed a formal tea transfer ceremony: she poured, they drank, they bowed, and the transfer of power was complete. Masru sat heavily in his front-row seat, his four guards beside him, with the magistrate at his side.

After the ceremonial dance, when moths destroyed five lanterns and the magistrate rushed back to the estate, Masru's priority was clear: "But I didn't get to eat yet." He was lured back to the festival by Boone and Ginkgo with a basket of fish, and proceeded to feast enthusiastically—grabbing food, sampling drinks from other people's hands, and complaining about how terrible the travel rations had been.

docs.title: Magistrate Kurosawa
docs.text: The dynamic is clearly strained. Kurosawa serves as Masru's advance guard, but appears frustrated by the mayor's lack of urgency. Masru, for his part, seems content to let the magistrate handle security while he enjoys himself.

docs.title: The Party
docs.text: Masru responded warmly when Boone and Ginkgo introduced themselves with food. He expressed interest in meeting the citizens of Willowshore: "We'll be here for some sustained time."

docs.title: Notable Quotes
docs.text:
"But I didn't get to eat yet."

"You can call me Mazrou. We don't need to be so formal."

"We'll be here for some sustained time."

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)
docs.text:
• Mentioned as the incoming mayor; arrival expected the next evening
  • The party was recruited by Magistrate Kurosawa to ensure his arrival goes smoothly

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• First appearance: Arrived during the Festival of Returning Spirits with four red-skinned oni guards
  • Physically unimposing—short for an oni, enormously round, unarmored
  • Completed the tea transfer ceremony with Mido, officially assuming power over Willowshore
  • Left the festival when the magistrate insisted after the moth incident, but was lured back by Boone and Ginkgo with food
  • Proved affable and approachable; introduced himself casually to the party
  • Feasted enthusiastically at the post-ceremony meal, sampling everything in sight

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Continued mingling at the festival after the ceremony—eating, drinking, and charming the townsfolk; described as "one of the crew" by the locals
  • His four guards remained present but silent and detached; they never ate or drank
  • Magistrate Kurosawa arrived at the festival, studied the crowd, then convinced Masru to retire for the evening; Masru was reluctant but acquiesced
  • Gave a brief, warm farewell to the townspeople: "I'm sure we'll have many more opportunities to get to know each other."
  • Departed with Kurosawa and guards to the estate
  • Co-signed the martial law decree alongside the magistrate—though Mido suggested Kurosawa is the one driving the investigation
  • Townspeople spoke mostly favorably of him after his departure, though some dismissed his friendliness as "day one of his act"

docs.title: Session Six (2026-03-19)
docs.text:
• Had the shared dream about the wounded land—described it identically to the party's experience; was "disturbed greatly" by it
  • Overrode Magistrate Kurosawa when a trooper summoned the party—pulled rank and insisted on meeting them despite the magistrate's ongoing investigation
  • Hired the party as "Masru's Faithful Five" with a formal contract: 10 gp daily stipend per person, incidentals by approval, and individual investigative licenses granting authority to act on his behalf
  • Their mission: investigate the dream, determine if the land is ailing, and report directly to him
  • Openly leered at Meilin, who was serving him in uncomfortably revealing attire; remarked that she could "make herself and myself very, very wealthy" working at the Orchid in Kirahata
  • Revealed his background from Kirahata—the "Vegas" of Chu Ye; former sumo competitor (nickname: "Sumo of Smiles"); ran several businesses there
  • Ate bread by biting loaves and putting them back on the shared plate; drank from a pitcher with two hands
  • Told the party the dream investigation is theirs to lead: "I'm really, you know, a manager."

docs.title: Session Eight (2026-04-22)
docs.text:
• Emerged from the estate after the demon attack; did not fight
  • Served as judge in the Rite of Open Reckoning invoked by Kurosawa
  • Heard prosecution and defense testimony; appeared reluctant throughout—openly uncomfortable watching Luda give her testimony, and visibly tired by the end
  • Did not intervene when Kurosawa cut off Yong's testimony mid-answer
  • Delivered the verdict: found the party guilty of unlawful interference, reckless endangerment, and spiritual disruption; ordered them held "until we can decide what to do"
  • His own investigative writs (the "Masru's Faithful Five" contract) did not shield the party from the verdict under his own rule
  • Kurosawa's closing compliment—"Wise Mayor"—felt less like praise and more like confirmation of who had actually won the room

docs.title: Meilin
docs.text: The gravekeeper who danced, and made a town forget she'd ever worn tatters.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Meilin Ancestry Unknown Occupation Gravekeeper Location Graveyard, Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Meilin is Willowshore's gravekeeper—normally seen in rough work clothes with matted hair, tending the cemetery grounds and maintaining the cricket cages that ward off spirits. She keeps a low profile and few in town pay her much attention.

That changed at the Festival of Returning Spirits, where she performed as one of five ceremonial dancers. After receiving a glow-up from Radiant Willow, Meilin stunned the entire town. Townspeople whispered that she might be the most beautiful young woman in Willowshore—a revelation that caught everyone by surprise except Willow, who had seen the potential all along.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text: Meilin is shy, reserved, and cautiously observant. She is grateful without being effusive, and measures her words carefully. When asked about the new mayor, she expressed guarded optimism while noting she "can't ignore the recent peculiarities"—a sign of quiet intelligence beneath her modest demeanor.

docs.title: Littlefinger
docs.text: Littlefinger helped Meilin at the graveyard during Session Two, assisting with the cricket cages after the undead encounter. Radiant Willow noticed their interaction and considers them a potential match. Sadly, Littlefinger was infiltrating the Yeshou estate during Meilin's dance performance and missed her transformation entirely.

docs.title: Radiant Willow
docs.text: Willow gave Meilin her makeover for the festival and is clearly proud of the result. She considers the gravekeeper one of her finest works.

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Performed as one of five dancers at the Festival of Returning Spirits ceremonial dance
  • Her transformation after Willow's makeover was the talk of the town—previously unnoticed, now considered possibly the most beautiful young woman in Willowshore
  • Littlefinger was absent during her performance (he was swimming through a koi pond tunnel at the time)

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Radiant Willow arranged for Meilin to stand next to Littlefinger at the festival—she was embarrassed but willing
  • Thanked the party again for helping during the undead crisis and for the graveyard cleanup
  • Spoke about the mayor: cautiously optimistic but reserving judgment—"I can't ignore some of the recent peculiarities. We had an undead uprising."
  • Described as having the "classic 80s rom-com glow-up"—her previously matted hair and rough clothes replaced by festival finery
  • Littlefinger expressed regret at missing her dance; she was pleased but still shy about the attention

docs.title: Session Six (2026-03-19)
docs.text:
• Served Mayor Masru at the estate in uncomfortable, revealing attire—a short-shorts crop-top style uniform; visibly embarrassed, tugging at the fabric to cover more
  • The mayor openly leered at her and made comments about her beauty and earning potential at a gentleman's club in Kirahata
  • Brought vellum and ink for the party's contract when summoned by the mayor's bell
  • Her current role appears to be some form of personal attendant to the mayor—an arrangement she seems deeply uncomfortable with

docs.title: Session Eight (2026-04-22)
docs.text:
• Called as the party's first defense witness during the Rite of Open Reckoning by Ginkgo
  • Testified quietly but clearly: the party had been "nothing but positive" in Willowshore; they did not cause the cemetery's undead uprising and in fact ran toward the undead to help the town
  • Admitted honestly that she could not speak to the cause of the chasm
  • Her testimony was the strongest unalloyed defense the party received all evening—measured, unflashy, and impossible for Kurosawa to dismantle without attacking a sympathetic local

docs.title: Mido
docs.text: The old woman smokes, drinks, and runs Willowshore's most powerful family from a borrowed room in a faded inn.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Mido Title Matriarch / Elder Ancestry Human (elderly) Affiliation Willowshore Ruling Family (Yeshou family) Location Silver Mist Lodge, Willowshore (temporarily)

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Mido is the matriarch of the Yeshou family—the de facto ruling family of Willowshore. She is elderly, possibly ancient, having been alive during the family's founding era. She holds court in a darkened suite at Silver Mist Lodge, curtains drawn, wreathed in tobacco smoke, flanked by two guards who are family retainers rather than blood relatives.

She has a piercing gaze and projects quiet, absolute authority. She drinks and smokes with the unhurried ease of someone who has outlasted most of her problems.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Mido is sharp, pragmatic, and honest when it serves her interests. She does not posture or waste words. When confronted with the party's evidence of her family's sabotage, she admitted it immediately—calling it a "foolish endeavor" by "more brash" members—rather than wasting time on denials.

She values honesty in return. Da Baishan's straightforward admission that the party was working under the magistrate's orders, but unwillingly, earned her respect and cooperation.

She is protective of her family's legacy and deeply opposed to the oni takeover, but she understands when a gambit has failed and cuts her losses cleanly.

docs.title: Goals
docs.text:
• Preserve the Yeshou family's influence in Willowshore—family has voted to stay permanently
  • Monitor Magistrate Kurosawa's activities, particularly his expeditions to the eastern ruins (Gosembiki)
  • Support the party's investigation while maintaining plausible deniability
  • Understand and counter the magistrate's arcane plans for Willowshore

docs.title: The Party
docs.text: Mido was impressed by the party's honesty and directness. She shared intelligence about Kurosawa's movements and honored the 100 gold payment to Hong as promised. She expressed hope for "further conversations and a deeper understanding" with the group.

docs.title: Magistrate Kurosawa
docs.text: She views him with deep suspicion. Her family has been tracking his movements and discovered his repeated visits to the Mourndusk Willow in the Dark Woods. She warned the party that he is "accomplished in the arcane arts" and "not exactly what he seems."

docs.title: Littlefinger
docs.text: She knows Littlefinger through his family connection. She apologized to him specifically for his injuries in the fire, calling them "scratches" with dry understatement.

docs.title: Hong
docs.text: She took advantage of Hong's desperation, hiring a child to do dangerous work. She acknowledged this was dishonorable and chose to honor the payment regardless.

docs.title: Notable Quotes
docs.text:
"I will give you the name. And it will—I'll say myself. I will take the blame for this endeavor."

"Like many things here in Tian Xia, [Kurosawa] is not exactly what he seems."

"I would venture in the daytime. The Dark Woods have their name because of the dangers in the dark."

docs.title: Mido's Half-Truth
docs.text:
The Order of the Palatine Eye's Book of Divided Testimony opened to the party's hand in Session Ten. The first of two book-truths it revealed concerned Mido directly:

Beware the elder who lies by subtraction.

She does not hide the road because she serves the dark.

She hides the road because she knows what followed the last traveler home.

A tyrant believes law can replace loyalty.

A geomancer believes lines can replace roots.

The reading is sharp and specific. Mido is not a collaborator. The Order does not accuse her of treachery; it accuses her of omission — of withholding information not because she favors Kurosawa's side but because the last person who was told whatever she has not told the party did not survive being told, and something followed them home.

The Order's testimony does not say who that traveler was. The party's working theories — none confirmed:

  • Cassian Voss, who was Order-marked, who is dead in Willowshore's graveyard, and who Mido did not warn in time
  • One of Cheng Yesho's prior comrades from the war that produced the smile he now wears
  • A previous head of the Yeshou family, whose disappearance might be exactly the reason the smuggling tunnels were dug in the first place

Until the party can ask Mido directly what she knows that the last traveler died for, the Order's reading of her stays where it is: a half-truth, lied by subtraction, lied for love.

docs.title: Session Two (2026-02-05)
docs.text:
• Introduced as the Yeshou family matriarch in Silver Mist Lodge
  • Admitted to sponsoring the warehouse sabotage via Hong
  • Promised to cancel all further planned disruptions to the festival
  • Honored the 100 gold payment to Hong
  • Revealed Kurosawa's secret visits to the Mourndusk Willow in the Dark Woods
  • Warned the party about Kurosawa's arcane abilities
  • Established a cautious rapport with the party

docs.title: Session Three (2026-02-12)
docs.text:
• Received Ginkgo and Donkey again for a second audience; served tea and rice cakes
  • Praised the party as "the young heroes of Willowshore"
  • Analyzed the ritual findings (dagger, bowl, lichen blight) and identified it as an old demonic summoning of the "Mother of a Thousand Wings"—an ancient demon of pestilence
  • Explained that Willowshore sits at a convergence of ley lines—arcane geomancy paths mirrored by the five rivers—creating a focal point for amplifying magical power
  • Revealed the Mourndusk Willow serves as a "lightning rod" for the ritual, reaching into ancient earth through its deep roots
  • Believes Kurosawa knows about the ley lines and that this is the true reason for his assignment to Willowshore
  • Admitted the ritual is beyond her knowledge to counter, but suggested two leads: searching the magistrate's quarters and exploring old mining towns in the southern mountains
  • Disclosed two secret entrances to the Yeshou estate: stables → wine cellar, and through the base of the koi pond
  • Mentioned the Festival of Returning Spirits is tomorrow evening; expects to see the party there

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Performed the tea transfer ceremony with Mayor Masru at the Seven Colored Songbird Theater, officially ceding power
  • Sat with her family on the right side of the front row; The Smiling One was present among the family
  • Locked eyes with Magistrate Kurosawa for a prolonged moment after the moth swarm destroyed five lanterns—both clearly understood its significance
  • Gathered her family and departed toward Silver Mist Lodge after the incident, slowed by townsfolk wanting to speak with her

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Received the party late at night after martial law was declared; served tea, smoking at her table
  • Revealed the Gosembiki (五芒星, the Five Pointed Star)—haunted ruins 10–20 miles east of Willowshore that Kurosawa has named and recently visited; described as a convergence point of arcane or chthonic intent
  • Her brother Cheng translated the Chthonic name for her
  • Confirmed the pattern of fives: five ley lines, five lanterns destroyed, and the Five Pointed Star; said she does not believe it is coincidence
  • Explained the prolonged eye-lock with Kurosawa at the festival as a mutual acknowledgment—"He knows that we are aware of his current festivities"
  • Warned the party that Kurosawa is "an elusive enigma of arcane energies"—vastly knowledgeable in the arcane arts
  • Agreed to provide an alibi for the party: the family will say they were present for a family vote during the festival, departing only when the alarm sounded
  • Revealed the family voted to stay in Willowshore permanently after the ceremony: "This place, we've been here for generations. We can't leave it to the hands of chance."
  • Littlefinger returned Cheng's military medal; Mido was appreciative but not leveraged by the gesture (Diplomacy 11)
  • Read as forthright and honest by Littlefinger (Perception—good roll)
  • Expressed belief that the land's spirits are unhappy: moths, infestations, and the ghoul uprising are signs of displeasure with the magistrate's activities

docs.title: Session Six (2026-03-19)
docs.text:
• Received Ginkgo's Message cantrip warning that Kurosawa was coming to confront her about the stolen items
  • Only one of her two guards was present—the other was later found 8+ miles east of town, transformed into an infected werewolf
  • Called Cheng in to speak with the party; exchanged a loaded glance with him before he answered about the old gods
  • Explained that five old gods existed before the current pantheon—overthrown but not destroyed, banished to a pocket dimension; their names are lost to history
  • Revealed the family has been sentenced to exile in five days by Kurosawa, who claims they stole items vital to the Imperium and the Council of the Magi
  • Smiled dryly when asked about escape plans, saying the family has discussed "contingency plans"
  • Said she slept peacefully and did not have the shared dream
  • Continues to provide the party's alibi at personal risk

docs.title: Session Nine (2026-05-07)
docs.text:
• Did not wait for Kurosawa's exile escort. Quietly relocated the family to a network of hideouts around Willowshore rather than the increasingly unsafe Silver Mist Inn
  • Sent her son Kuji to extract the imprisoned party through the family's old smuggling tunnels beneath the warehouses; greeted them at a hidden cave outcropping west of town from a rickshaw, blanket across her legs, with her surviving guard, Kuji, and her daughter
  • Read Kurosawa's recovered note (The Note of Five Keys); confirmed her long-held suspicion that he was "a geomancer by studies, by arts, and very interested in the Gosembiki ley line", and admitted "it's even worse than I may have thought"
  • Provided the lore on the Hollow of Seven Cedars (the old Lovers' Grove) — the dream-eater spirit, the seven cedars, the trinkets, and the pool — and explained Kurosawa's likely intent to invert the spirit's function and rewrite the surviving townspeople's memory
  • Confirmed her own knowledge has limits: she could not interpret all five sites in the note, and could not advise the party on how to face the magistrate's geomancy directly
  • Refused to flee Willowshore entirely. "We do not want to stray far. Willowshore is still our home, and we will look to find ways of usurping this Kurosawa before he can do more damage."
  • Provisioned the party out of the family's stashes — two healer's kits, a sized full plate for Boone, a sling and five healing potions; said the family could not provide a heavy plate for Da Baishan's frame on short notice, but offered to try
  • Pledged the family's spy and tunnel network to keep watching Kurosawa from the shadows while the party traveled south to the Order of the Palatine Eye
  • Wished them luck; her smile was small, and she did not stand

docs.title: Session Ten (2026-05-14)
docs.text:
• Did not appear in person. Implicated by the Order from a distance: the Book of Divided Testimony named her in the second of its eight reveals as the elder who lies by subtraction, hiding the road "because she knows what followed the last traveler home"
  • The party — particularly Boone, the only one who can read the book — now reads her past helpfulness through this lens: not duplicity, but cautious omission against a danger she would not name aloud
  • The next time the party returns to Willowshore is now scheduled to include a direct question to Mido about what she has not been telling them, and who paid the price the last time it was told

docs.title: The Blue-Skinned Administrator
docs.text: No weapons, no armor, no name yet — and even the guard captain follows her orders.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Unknown Ancestry Oni (blue-skinned) Role Administrator / arcanist (?) at the Yeshou estate Affiliation Shogun Tsuneni's regime — arrived with the reinforced garrison Location Willowshore, operating out of the occupied Yeshou estate Status Active; observed only from a distance (Session Twelve)

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
A small blue-skinned oni woman, perhaps 5'6" — nothing like Kurosawa's seven-foot menace or the Mayor's girth. She carries no visible weapons: no nodachi, no katana like the red-skinned guardsmen wear. The party's read from the tree line was "maybe a wizard, maybe an accountant" — bookkeeping, paperwork, quiet authority.

What she lacks in stature she holds in rank. She moves with at least one guard escort, works out of the estate, and issues orders that are obeyed — even Captain Akoto follows them. She is the third blue-skinned oni seen in Willowshore, and like the other two, the red-skinned soldiery answers to her.

docs.title: Context of Her Arrival
docs.text:
The party spotted her while surveilling Willowshore from the northwest tree line (Session Twelve), after roughly a month away:

  • The garrison has doubled — approximately eight red-skinned oni guards, up from four (one of whom died in the demon attack)
  • Kurosawa was nowhere to be seen across hours of observation — a conspicuous absence, given that she now appears to run the estate he occupied
  • The town otherwise looked eerily normal, going about its recovering business

Whether she is Kurosawa's subordinate, his replacement, his watcher, or something else is unknown.

docs.title: The Party's Read
docs.text: Boone, on no evidence whatsoever: "I think she's a potential ally, guys. I feel it. I think she's a good one." Da Baishan: "You just think she's pretty." Boone: "Often go hand in hand."

docs.title: Open Questions
docs.text:
• Who is she, and what is her relationship to Kurosawa? Deputy, auditor, successor?
  • Where is Kurosawa? Is her presence connected to his absence — and does either know yet that his chambers were robbed?
  • What is she administering? The doubled garrison suggests the regime is tightening its grip on Willowshore — in response to the party's escape, the chasm, or something else
  • Is Boone right?

docs.title: Session Twelve (2026-07-16)
docs.text:
• First observed from the tree line northwest of Willowshore during the party's pre-heist surveillance
  • Noted commanding guards (including Captain Akoto) from the Yeshou estate, unarmed, in Kurosawa's absence
  • Not encountered directly; the party's estate heist that night avoided all contact

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Peony Switchstep Ancestry Halfling Occupation Seamstress Location Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Peony Switchstep is a halfling seamstress in Willowshore, known for her cheerful disposition and love of community gossip. Radiant Willow considers her an ideal match for Da Baishan—reasoning that his stern nature would be complemented by someone who sees the best in everyone.

docs.title: Da Baishan (potential)
docs.text: Willow has been trying to arrange a meeting. Peony has been visibly reluctant—giving Willow resistance when she tried to bring her over to meet Da Baishan at the festival.

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text: • Mentioned by Radiant Willow as a matchmaking candidate for Da Baishan

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text: • Present at the festival; Willow attempted to bring her to meet Da Baishan but she gave visible resistance

docs.title: Radiant Willow
docs.text: "Everything is a canvas for beauty."

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Radiant Willow (prefers "Willow") Ancestry Leshy (Flower) Occupation Beautician, stylist, spa owner Location Dew Drop Petals, Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Radiant Willow is a young, beautiful flower leshy who practically radiates enthusiasm. She runs Dew Drop Petals, a small salon and spa in Willowshore where she offers manicures, pedicures, hair styling, beard braiding, and fashion consulting. She's known for her singing and dancing, and will be performing at the Festival of Returning Spirits.

When she enters a room, she brings energy with her—skipping, singing lullabies, and immediately assessing everyone's beauty potential. She sees the world as a canvas waiting to be adorned.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Willow is irrepressibly cheerful, effusive, and persistent. She doesn't take "no" for an answer easily—at least not when it comes to offering her services. She believes passionately that everyone deserves to look beautiful, and she cannot understand why anyone would choose to be "drab."

Despite her bubbly exterior, she's an excellent listener. As she styles hair and files nails, people talk to her—and she hears all the gossip in town. She was coy about sharing what she knows, but hinted that a visit to her shop might loosen her tongue.

docs.title: Views on the Oni
docs.text: Willow is cautiously optimistic about the incoming mayor. She hopes the change might bring prosperity and give Willowshore something to smile about. She admires Magistrate Kurosawa's "gorgeous blue skin" but wishes he would smile more. She's reserving judgment until Mayor Masru actually arrives.

docs.title: Ginkgo
docs.text: Willow was delighted to meet a fellow leshy and responded warmly to his flattery. She scheduled him for a full makeover the morning before the festival, clearly excited at the prospect of working with fungal aesthetics.

docs.title: The Party
docs.text: She tried (and mostly failed) to interest the others in her services: - Da Baishan flatly refused to adorn his tusks - Boone was noncommittal about beard braiding - Littlefinger declined hair styling but expressed interest in pedicures

docs.title: Services
docs.text:
Dew Drop Petals offers:

  • Hair styling and braiding
  • Manicures and pedicures
  • Beard styling (she desperately wants to braid Boone's)
  • Tusk adornment (she desperately wants to cap Da Baishan's with rubies)
  • Fashion consulting
  • Cucumber water (complimentary)

docs.title: Notable Quotes
docs.text:
"Now, if this isn't the most interesting group I've ever seen!"

"Littlefinger, your hands are dreadful. We could do wonders to soften those for you."

"It is manly to look beautiful."

"I will get you to adorn your tusks one day." — To Da Baishan

docs.title: Matchmaking
docs.text:
Willow considers herself an innate matchmaker—she believes finding love connections is one of her gifts, alongside dance and beauty. She has identified several potential pairings among the party and townsfolk:

Match Her Reasoning Da Baishan & Peony Switchstep "He's stern; she sees the best in everyone. Can you imagine the wedding on the bridge?" Boone & Cloud Ygritte "She's sweet, patient, likes trading stories. He's from a faraway land. And she's got the most adorable chin fuzz." Littlefinger & Meilin She noticed Littlefinger spent time helping Meilin at the graveyard and planned to watch his reaction to her dance

She recruited Ginkgo as her accomplice, planning to arrange "accidental" meetings at the festival feast.

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)
docs.text:
• Introduced at Silver Mist Lodge, immediately trying to drum up business
  • Scheduled Ginkgo for a makeover the next morning
  • Hinted she hears gossip from her customers
  • Mentioned she'll perform a dance at the festival
  • Attempted to recruit the entire party for beauty treatments

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Gave Ginkgo a peach petal exfoliation and wove flower petals into his fungal exterior; treated Donkey to a similar session
  • Revealed matchmaking plans for Da Baishan, Boone, and Littlefinger with local women
  • Recruited Ginkgo as her matchmaking accomplice for the festival feast
  • Shared information about Cassian Voss: he told her about "five lines, five fortunes" converging at Willowshore and that "something is being drawn here"; he planned to head east following the ley lines
  • Gave fortune petals to Ginkgo and Donkey on their way out
  • Performed in the Festival of Returning Spirits ceremonial dance—graceful and radiant, a standout among the five dancers
  • Presented chrysanthemum blooms to Mayor Masru and Elder Mido to close the performance
  • Responded warmly to Ginkgo's romantic interest; agreed to get a drink together later

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Executed her matchmaking plan at the festival feast: placed Meilin next to Littlefinger and toasted "to friendship and new beginnings"
  • Attempted to bring Peony Switchstep to meet Da Baishan but met visible resistance
  • Cloud Ygritte (Boone's match) had already retired for the evening before introductions could be made

docs.title: Session Six (2026-03-19)
docs.text:
• Kurosawa visited her shop that morning, aggressively questioning her about the party—especially Ginkgo, Littlefinger, and Donkey; she described him as "an angry person" who is "beginning to get me to question that most people are good"
  • Confirmed she had the shared dream: vivid, intense, with the taste of earth and river water; she normally has "very mild happy dreams" and was disturbed
  • Interpreted the dream as Willowshore being sick—"The spirits of Willowshore are damaged. They're calling out for help."
  • Noted the dream's five lines reminded her of the rivers near Willowshore; said the river "doesn't seem to sing as loudly as it did before" and the ground is "matted and tired"
  • Spritzed Ginkgo and Donkey with a new perfume
  • Attempted to match Boone with someone new; Boone politely declined
  • Business is slow under the lockdown; she appreciated the party's visit and asked them to return

docs.title: Session Eight (2026-04-22)
docs.text:
• Present in the crowd during the Rite of Open Reckoning
  • Boone appealed to the whole town: "Who had the dream?"
  • In a square full of silent, nervous townsfolk who looked around at one another and did nothing, Willow was the only person to raise her hand
  • Spoke briefly about the dream: the land sick, the rivers silent, a wound in the earth
  • Her testimony was dismissed by Kurosawa as "visions and dreams on trial", but her courage stood alone and was noticed
  • Retroactively her quiet flower-leshy instinct for beauty and care ("the spirits of Willowshore are calling out for help") aligns her firmly with Vujravati's dream-voice, the same voice the party has now met directly

docs.title: Session Nine (2026-05-07)
docs.text:
• Stopped a battle with words. As Kurosawa and his oni were finishing the party off — Boone at 0 HP, Littlefinger dying on the ground, an earthen wall pinning the rest — Willow ran out of town toward the fight, hands open, calling: "No, stop the ugliness. No more bloodshed. Please. Our town has sacrificed enough. No more killing."
  • Kurosawa raised his sword toward her, then lowered it. He hesitated for her where he had hesitated for no one else. The party survived because she walked into a battlefield unarmed and asked them to stop.
  • Knelt beside the wounded party and held her hand on Boone: "No more. Just please, everybody stop. We need beauty here. We need peace."
  • Was implicitly the moral cover Kurosawa needed to take the party alive rather than execute them — Mido and Boone both understood her courage as the reason none of the party died that night
  • Boone, before agreeing to surrender: "Willow, you showed bravery during our trial. Out of respect for you, I will talk peacefully."
